message.content: Adversaries may forge web cookies to gain unauthorized access to web applications or Internet services. Unlike stealing session cookies, this technique involves generating new cookies using secret values such as passwords or cryptographic keys. These forged cookies can bypass multi-factor authentication and other security measures, allowing adversaries to access sensitive resources. Common exploitation methods include leveraging documented cookie values and using tools to generate the necessary cookies. A notable example is APT29, which bypassed MFA on OWA accounts by generating cookies from stolen secret keys. Mitigation strategies include regularly rotating cryptographic keys, implementing robust monitoring for unusual cookie generation activities, and employing additional layers of authentication and authorization checks beyond standard session cookies.
